High risk of recurrence
Gasping syndrome in preterm infants
Neuromuscular blocking potential; may worsen muscle weakness

Category B; use if benefit outweighs risk
Excreted in milk; may cause infant diarrhea and candidiasis

Aminoglycosides are excreted in milk; infant risk of ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity

contraindications.contraindication_name: active hemoptysis or recent massive hemoptysis
contraindications.reason: Inhalation may worsen airway bleeding

contraindications.contraindication_name: severe renal impairment (systemic absorption may cause nephrotoxicity)
contraindications.reason: Serum levels should be monitored; risk of toxicity

contraindications.contraindication_name: existing hearing loss or vestibular dysfunction
contraindications.reason: Aminoglycoside ototoxicity may cause irreversible hearing loss

contraindications.contraindication_name: myasthenia gravis or other neuromuscular disorders
contraindications.reason: Aminoglycosides can potentiate neuromuscular blockade

contraindications.contraindication_name: active asthma exacerbation or bronchospasm at time of inhalation
contraindications.reason: Inhalation may trigger bronchospasm; pre-treatment with bronchodilator recommended
